title: bridge-slack: timestamp replay check overflows for extreme attacker-sent ts
description: bridge-slack/src/slack.rs:96: `(now - ts).abs() > 300` — if an attacker sends X-Slack-Request-Timestamp: -9223372036854775808, the subtraction `now - ts` overflows i64 in debug mode (panic) or wraps in release mode (wrong result). The HMAC check that follows would still reject the forged request, but the overflow happens before it. Fix: replace with saturating arithmetic: `now.saturating_sub(ts).abs() > 300` ensures no overflow for any attacker-supplied ts.
